state.title: bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs

part.text:
The bug is in `addItem` function. It reassigns `const next = state`, making `next` reference the same object as `state`. When `next.items.push(item)` is called, it mutates the original state.

The fix is to create a copy of the object and copy the array to avoid shared references:

input.description: Run test to verify the fix

state.title: Run test to verify the fix

part.text: The test expects `next.total === 1` but it's 0 because I used `state.items.length` before the item was added. I should use `next.items.length`:

metadata.diff:
Index: /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-23T16-47-14-546Z/tokenwarden-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
===================================================================
--- /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-23T16-47-14-546Z/tokenwarden-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
+++ /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-23T16-47-14-546Z/tokenwarden-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
@@ -5,7 +5,7 @@
 export function addItem(state, item) {
   const next = { ...state }
   next.items = [...state.items]
   next.items.push(item)
-  next.total = state.items.length
+  next.total = next.items.length
   return next
 }

filediff.patch:
Index: /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-23T16-47-14-546Z/tokenwarden-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
===================================================================
--- /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-23T16-47-14-546Z/tokenwarden-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
+++ /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-23T16-47-14-546Z/tokenwarden-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
@@ -5,7 +5,7 @@
 export function addItem(state, item) {
   const next = { ...state }
   next.items = [...state.items]
   next.items.push(item)
-  next.total = state.items.length
+  next.total = next.items.length
   return next
 }

state.title: Run test to verify the fix

part.text:
Test passes. Final change was minimal - just two edits:
1. Added `const next = { ...state }` instead of `const next = state` to avoid mutating the original object
2. Changed `next.items = [...state.items]` to ensure a new array copy
3. Changed `next.total = state.items.length` to `next.total = next.items.length` to compute total after the item was added
